title: Decreasing VLCC mileage likely to continue || Aframax outlook bleak in the Atlantic Basin
section_path: Decreasing VLCC mileage likely to continue || Aframax outlook bleak in the Atlantic Basin
section_path_text: Decreasing VLCC mileage likely to continue || Aframax outlook bleak in the Atlantic Basin
summary:
Main
This week, in the East, we discuss falling VLCC average voyage mileage and the outlook for MEG LR freight rates. Over in the West, we focus on the changing fortunes of Aframaxes in the Atlantic Basin, and we examine why TC2 is failing to ignite despite vessel supply constraints. By Mary Melton
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-04-11 103632.png | assets/2024-04-12_decreasing-vlcc-mileage-likely-to-continue-aframax-outlook-bleak-in-the-atlantic_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_e017433986c7.png
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-04-11 103653.png | assets/2024-04-12_decreasing-vlcc-mileage-likely-to-continue-aframax-outlook-bleak-in-the-atlantic_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_da818eeb31d5.png
VLCCs are travelling shorter distances as a result of less Atlantic Basin crudes going to Northeast Asia.
